purpose: Stable host-machine bridge for Abyss OS and future abyss-stack integration.

contract.stack_dependency_direction: abyss-stack may consume abyss-machine; abyss-machine must not import or mutate abyss-stack

storage_policy.principle: Keep stable configs/manifests/small state and hook contracts on /; route large mutable caches, generated AI artifacts, host runtimes, and agent scratch data directly to {{ABYSS_MACHINE_SRV}}. Store durable facts and low-level snapshots under {{ABYSS_MACHINE_STATE}}. Do not leave symlink tails unless explicitly approved.

podman_preflight.method: direct rootless Podman storage.conf graphroot migration; no symlink tail

podman_graphroot.policy: active container runtime storage; preserve named volumes and bind mounts; no symlink tail

dictation.npu_status: installed and usable, but not primary for current STT because measured Whisper large latency is slower than GPU

replacements.purpose: host-side project vocabulary and transcript cleanup without code edits

journal.retention: text and metadata are durable; runtime WAV files are not copied by default

profiles.auto: duration-aware selector: long for long-form speech, quality otherwise; command intent is detected after transcription by trigger phrase
profiles.long: large-v3-turbo with softer VAD and punctuation
profiles.command: manual short phrase decoding profile; not auto-selected by duration

command_intent.payload_contract: transcripts whose text starts with a configured trigger expose intent.type=command and intent.payload

hotkeys.action: record and paste into normal text fields
record and paste into terminal-style fields

runtime_toggles.ABYSS_DICTATION_AUDIO_PREPROCESS: 1 by default; digital RMS normalization and edge cleanup
runtime_toggles.ABYSS_DICTATION_VAD_SEGMENTS: 1 by default; split long recordings on pauses
runtime_toggles.ABYSS_DICTATION_PUNCTUATION_STYLE: normal by default; soft for long profile

observability.policy: low-overhead periodic host telemetry; profiling and high-frequency tracing are out of scope

commands.tts_synth_quality: abyss-machine ai tts synth --profile quality --text TEXT --json
commands.tts_synth_quality_compact: abyss-machine ai tts synth --profile quality-compact --text TEXT --json
commands.tts_server_run_quality_compact: abyss-machine ai tts server run --profile quality-compact

ai_host.policy: read-only by default host AI inventory, eval evidence, capability registry, thermal policy, storage hygiene, runtime lifecycle, workload cost statistics, CPU routed-heavy policy, and support bundles; launchers and model orchestration belong in abyss-stack later

tts.compare_default: reads latest successful per-profile evals without model execution; use --run for fresh heavy evals

warm_server.policy_gate: ExecCondition uses abyss-machine ai policy --json .can_run_heavy; warm GPU TTS does not use routed CPU heavy.

latest_manual_measurement.note: host measurement under current battery/thermal conditions; facts-only, not a stack promotion

ai_devices.npu_driver_note: Fedora package remains installed, but ldconfig prefers the newer Intel user-mode driver in /opt.

integration_notes: Scan {{ABYSS_MACHINE_ETC}}/bridge.json first.
Read {{ABYSS_MACHINE_ETC}}/AGENTS.md for host-layer orientation.
Read {{ABYSS_MACHINE_ETC}}/storage-policy.json before writing large caches, model downloads, generated AI artifacts, or runtime environments.
Read {{ABYSS_MACHINE_ETC}}/commands.md for common operator commands and tunables.
Use abyss-machine bridge --json to confirm command availability.
Use abyss-machine facts --json for durable host facts.
Use abyss-machine doctor --json for host readiness and warnings.
Use abyss-machine status --json for current thermal, battery, power and AI-device state.
Use abyss-machine mode status --json for the current Abyss work-mode overlay.
Use abyss-machine snapshots --json for Btrfs/Snapper coverage and rollback notes.
Use abyss-machine ai status --json for local AI host topology, devices, model inventory and latest benchmark state.
Use {{ABYSS_MACHINE_STATE}}/ai/AGENTS.md before wiring agents or abyss-stack into local AI hardware.
Use abyss-machine ai benchmark --quick --json for a low-cost OpenVINO CPU/GPU/NPU smoke check.
Use abyss-machine ai eval --quick --json for bounded real host evals across STT, embeddings and local text generation.
Use abyss-machine ai capabilities --json to discover executable host AI capabilities and stack bridge hints.
Use abyss-machine ai policy --json before heavy local AI work to respect thermal, battery and work-mode state.
Use ai policy can_run_heavy for unrestricted heavy work; use can_run_routed_heavy plus abyss-machine ai cpu route --class heavy --json for CPU-backed work around a narrow single-core hotspot.
Use abyss-machine ai cpu route --class medium --json before CPU-backed local AI, compiler, or agent workloads; it routes around hot cores without globally disabling CPU use.
Use abyss-machine ai cpu thermal-map --json when investigating single-core hotspots.
Use abyss-machine ai storage --json to inspect model storage and generated cache hygiene.
Prefer {{ABYSS_MACHINE_SRV}}/cache and {{ABYSS_MACHINE_SRV}}/runtimes for large generated host data; update configs/wrappers to direct paths instead of leaving compatibility symlinks.
Use abyss-machine ai runtime --json to inspect OpenVINO, NPU, GPU and package lifecycle state.
Use abyss-machine ai report --json for one compact AI support bundle for future agents.
Use abyss-machine ai tts inventory --json for host-managed TTS model/runtime facts.
Use abyss-machine ai tts profiles --json before connecting abyss-stack TTS launchers; it reports quality, quality-compact, NPU experimental and fallback bridge readiness.
Use abyss-machine ai tts eval --profile quality --json only when policy allows heavy GPU work or the operator explicitly forces validation.
Use abyss-machine ai tts eval --profile quality-compact --json for the lower-memory Qwen3-TTS OpenVINO GPU validation path.
Use abyss-machine ai tts synth --profile quality --text TEXT --json for host-owned Qwen3-TTS WAV generation; cold one-shot runs are validation/offline generation, not the final warm interactive UX.
Use abyss-machine ai workload --json for host workload classes, current route advice and measured cost statistics.
Use abyss-machine ai workload stats --json for append-only eval/benchmark-derived measurements; absent metrics mean unmeasured.
Use abyss-machine ai workload refresh --json for safe probe-only automatic refresh; it never runs real eval suites.
Heavy AI eval commands are policy-gated; use --force only for explicit operator-controlled validation.
Use abyss-machine observability status --json for thermal/battery telemetry status and current log paths.
Use {{ABYSS_MACHINE_STATE}}/observability/AGENTS.md before investigating telemetry history directly.
Use abyss-machine dictation status --json for local STT model and hotkey-readiness.
Use abyss-machine dictation journal paths --json for durable transcript journal topology.
Use abyss-machine dictation journal latest --json for the latest persisted voice transcript event.
Use abyss-machine dictation journal tail --lines 20 for recent readable voice transcript entries.
Use abyss-machine dictation audio-doctor --json for microphone/source diagnostics.
Use abyss-machine dictation config get --json for effective dictation settings.
Use abyss-machine dictation profile list --json for STT profile contracts and auto-selection policy.
Use abyss-machine dictation replacements list --json for host-side vocabulary cleanup rules.
Use abyss-machine dictation intent test TEXT --json to verify command trigger parsing.
Do not treat this host layer as an abyss-stack runtime component.
Use abyss-machine ai tts voices --json for concise voice/profile contracts.
Use abyss-machine ai tts compare --json for last successful per-profile TTS evidence without rerunning heavy model evals; add --run only for explicit fresh evals.
Use {{ABYSS_MACHINE_STATE}}/ai/tts/evals/success and latest_success_by_profile.json for profile-indexed TTS eval evidence.
Use abyss-machine ai tts server status --json before assuming the warm TTS service is active.
The user service abyss-tts-server.service is enabled but policy-gated; it starts only when abyss-machine ai policy reports can_run_heavy=true.
Use abyss-machine stack-bridge --json as the read-only host-to-stack handoff bundle for future abyss-stack consumers.
Use abyss-machine stack-bridge validate --json before wiring stack-side bridge code to host-machine artifacts.
Use {{ABYSS_MACHINE_ETC}}/STACK-BRIDGE.md and {{ABYSS_MACHINE_STATE}}/stack-bridge/AGENTS.md for stack bridge orientation.
Use abyss-machine mode paths --json and {{ABYSS_MACHINE_STATE}}/mode/AGENTS.md for work-mode orientation.
Use abyss-machine mode plan --json as the host-side bridge for power profile, cooling target, thermal gate, storage/process context and launch policy.
Use abyss-machine processes game-guard --json as the read-only active-game bridge; never scrape or mutate /srv/games directly.
When game_guard.active=true, future stack launchers should defer unattended medium-or-heavier work and default heavy/sustained work unless explicitly operator-forced.
notes: Use abyss-machine enter --json as the compact first entry surface for future agents.
Host-layer change records live under {{ABYSS_MACHINE_STATE}}/changes and do not replace project repository history.
Use abyss-machine topology validate --json after topology, bridge, path, or change-ledger edits.
Use abyss-machine mode plan --json before starting heavy local AI, compiler, benchmark, or sustained agent work.
Use abyss-machine mode validate --json after changing power, cooling, AI launch policy, or mode documentation.
Use abyss-machine processes game-guard --json before heavy local AI, compiler, benchmark, or sustained agent work when games may be running.
Game guard separates active games from Steam/platform helpers and blocks default heavy/sustained ai cpu launch work without mutating game processes.

mode_host.contract: GNOME power profiles select saver/balanced/performance; Abyss Machine adds cooling, thermal gates, battery saver enforcement, AI overlay and launch policy.
mode_host.battery_policy: while discharging, effective mode is saver and selected AC mode is preserved for restore
mode_host.thermal_policy: hot/critical escalates cooling target and gates new unattended heavy work without killing running operator tasks; CPU-backed operator work can use launch_policy.cpu_routed_heavy when the hotspot is narrow and package heat is safe
non_claims: ai is a host overlay, not a fourth GNOME panel power-profile entry
mode plan does not start workloads; callers must consume the launch policy explicitly
mode routes do not mutate abyss-stack or project repositories
cpu_routed_heavy is an explicit route bridge, not permission to run unrestricted GPU/NPU heavy work

process_bridge.contract: Process bridge is facts-first and must not kill, throttle, or re-affinitize existing user/game processes.
